members.name: AddFilter
members.signature: public void AddFilter(String filter, String description, String mimeType)
members.summary: Adds a comma-separated file extension [param filter] and comma-separated MIME type [param mime_type] option to the [FileDialog] with an optional [param description], which restricts what files can be picked. A [param filter] should be of the form [code]"filename.extension"[/code], where filename and extension can be [code]*[/code] to match any string. Filters starting with [code].[/code] (i.e. empty filenames) are not allowed. For example, a [param filter] of [code]"*.png, *.jpg"[/code], a [param mime_type] of [code]image/png, image/jpeg[/code], and a [param description] of [code]"Images"[/code] results in filter text "Images (*.png, *.jpg)". [b]Note:[/b] Embedded file dialogs and Windows file dialogs support only file extensions, while Android, Linux, and macOS file dialogs also support MIME types.

members.name: AddOption
members.signature: public void AddOption(String name, PackedStringArray values, int defaultValueIndex)
members.summary: Adds an additional [OptionButton] to the file dialog. If [param values] is empty, a [CheckBox] is added instead. [param default_value_index] should be an index of the value in the [param values]. If [param values] is empty it should be either [code]1[/code] (checked), or [code]0[/code] (unchecked).

members.name: ClearFilenameFilter
members.summary: Clear the filter for file names.

members.name: ClearFilters
members.summary: Clear all the added filters in the dialog.

members.name: DeselectAll
members.summary: Clear all currently selected items in the dialog.

members.name: GetFavoriteList
members.summary: Returns the list of favorite directories, which is shared by all [FileDialog] nodes. Useful to store the list of favorites between project sessions. This method can be called only from the main thread.

members.name: GetLineEdit
members.summary: Returns the LineEdit for the selected file. [b]Warning:[/b] This is a required internal node, removing and freeing it may cause a crash. If you wish to hide it or any of its children, use their [member CanvasItem.visible] property.

members.name: GetOptionDefault
members.summary: Returns the default value index of the [OptionButton] or [CheckBox] with index [param option].

members.name: GetOptionName
members.summary: Returns the name of the [OptionButton] or [CheckBox] with index [param option].

members.name: GetOptionValues
members.summary: Returns an array of values of the [OptionButton] with index [param option].

members.name: GetRecentList
members.summary: Returns the list of recent directories, which is shared by all [FileDialog] nodes. Useful to store the list of recents between project sessions. This method can be called only from the main thread.

members.name: GetSelectedOptions
members.summary: Returns a [Dictionary] with the selected values of the additional [OptionButton]s and/or [CheckBox]es. [Dictionary] keys are names and values are selected value indices.

members.name: GetVbox
members.summary: Returns the vertical box container of the dialog, custom controls can be added to it. [b]Warning:[/b] This is a required internal node, removing and freeing it may cause a crash. If you wish to hide it or any of its children, use their [member CanvasItem.visible] property. [b]Note:[/b] Changes to this node are ignored by native file dialogs, use [method add_option] to add custom elements to the dialog instead.

members.name: Invalidate
members.summary: Invalidates and updates this dialog's content list. [b]Note:[/b] This method does nothing on native file dialogs.

members.name: IsCustomizationFlagEnabled
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the provided [param flag] is enabled.

members.name: PopupFileDialog
members.summary: Shows the [FileDialog] using the default size and position for file dialogs, and selects the file name if there is a current file.

members.name: SetCustomizationFlagEnabled
members.signature: public void SetCustomizationFlagEnabled(int flag, bool enabled)
members.summary: Sets the specified customization [param flag], allowing to customize the features available in this [FileDialog].

members.name: SetFavoriteList
members.summary: Sets the list of favorite directories, which is shared by all [FileDialog] nodes. Useful to restore the list of favorites saved with [method get_favorite_list]. This method can be called only from the main thread. [b]Note:[/b] [FileDialog] will update its internal [ItemList] of favorites when its visibility changes. Be sure to call this method earlier if you want your changes to have effect.

members.name: SetGetIconCallback
members.summary: Sets the callback used by the [FileDialog] nodes to get a file icon, when [constant DISPLAY_LIST] mode is used. The callback should take a single [String] argument (file path), and return a [Texture2D]. If an invalid texture is returned, the [theme_item file] icon will be used instead.

members.name: SetGetThumbnailCallback
members.summary: Sets the callback used by the [FileDialog] nodes to get a file icon, when [constant DISPLAY_THUMBNAILS] mode is used. The callback should take a single [String] argument (file path), and return a [Texture2D]. If an invalid texture is returned, the [theme_item file_thumbnail] icon will be used instead. Thumbnails are usually more complex and may take a while to load. To avoid stalling the application, you can use [ImageTexture] to asynchronously create the thumbnail. [codeblock] func _ready(): FileDialog.set_get_thumbnail_callback(thumbnail_method) func thumbnail_method(path): var image_texture = ImageTexture.new() make_thumbnail_async(path, image_texture) return image_texture func make_thumbnail_async(path, image_texture): var thumbnail_texture = await generate_thumbnail(path) # Some method that generates a thumbnail. image_texture.set_image(thumbnail_texture.get_image()) [/codeblock]

members.name: SetOptionDefault
members.signature: public void SetOptionDefault(int option, int defaultValueIndex)
members.summary: Sets the default value index of the [OptionButton] or [CheckBox] with index [param option].

members.name: SetOptionName
members.signature: public void SetOptionName(int option, String name)
members.summary: Sets the name of the [OptionButton] or [CheckBox] with index [param option].

members.name: SetOptionValues
members.signature: public void SetOptionValues(int option, PackedStringArray values)
members.summary: Sets the option values of the [OptionButton] with index [param option].

members.name: SetRecentList
members.summary: Sets the list of recent directories, which is shared by all [FileDialog] nodes. Useful to restore the list of recents saved with [method set_recent_list]. This method can be called only from the main thread. [b]Note:[/b] [FileDialog] will update its internal [ItemList] of recent directories when its visibility changes. Be sure to call this method earlier if you want your changes to have effect.

members.name: Access
members.summary: The file system access scope. [b]Warning:[/b] In Web builds, FileDialog cannot access the host file system. In sandboxed Linux and macOS environments, [member use_native_dialog] is automatically used to allow limited access to host file system.

members.name: CurrentDir
members.summary: The current working directory of the file dialog. [b]Note:[/b] For native file dialogs, this property is only treated as a hint and may not be respected by specific OS implementations.

members.name: CurrentFile
members.summary: The currently selected file of the file dialog.

members.name: CurrentPath
members.summary: The currently selected file path of the file dialog.

members.name: DeletingEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the context menu will show the "Delete" option, which allows moving files and folders to trash.

members.name: DisplayMode
members.summary: Display mode of the dialog's file list.

members.name: FavoritesEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], shows the toggle favorite button and favorite list on the left side of the dialog.

members.name: FileFilterToggleEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], shows the toggle file filter button.

members.name: FileMode
members.summary: The dialog's open or save mode, which affects the selection behavior.

members.name: FileSortOptionsEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], shows the file sorting options button.

members.name: FilenameFilter
members.summary: The filter for file names (case-insensitive). When set to a non-empty string, only files that contains the substring will be shown. [member filename_filter] can be edited by the user with the filter button at the top of the file dialog. See also [member filters], which should be used to restrict the file types that can be selected instead of [member filename_filter] which is meant to be set by the user.

members.name: Filters
members.summary: The available file type filters. Each filter string in the array should be formatted like this: [code]*.png,*.jpg,*.jpeg;Image Files;image/png,image/jpeg[/code]. The description text of the filter is optional and can be omitted. Both file extensions and MIME type should be always set. [b]Note:[/b] Embedded file dialogs and Windows file dialogs support only file extensions, while Android, Linux, and macOS file dialogs also support MIME types.

members.name: FolderCreationEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], shows the button for creating new directories (when using [constant FILE_MODE_OPEN_DIR], [constant FILE_MODE_OPEN_ANY], or [constant FILE_MODE_SAVE_FILE]), and the context menu will have the "New Folder..." option.

members.name: HiddenFilesToggleEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], shows the toggle hidden files button.

members.name: LayoutToggleEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], shows the layout switch buttons (list/thumbnails).

members.name: ModeOverridesTitle
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], changing the [member file_mode] property will set the window title accordingly (e.g. setting [member file_mode] to [constant FILE_MODE_OPEN_FILE] will change the window title to "Open a File").

members.name: OptionCount
members.summary: The number of additional [OptionButton]s and [CheckBox]es in the dialog.

members.name: OverwriteWarningEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the [FileDialog] will warn the user before overwriting files in save mode.

members.name: RecentListEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], shows the recent directories list on the left side of the dialog.

members.name: RootSubfolder
members.summary: If non-empty, the given sub-folder will be "root" of this [FileDialog], i.e. user won't be able to go to its parent directory. [b]Note:[/b] This property is ignored by native file dialogs.

members.name: ShowHiddenFiles
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the dialog will show hidden files. [b]Note:[/b] This property is ignored by native file dialogs on Android and Linux.

members.name: UseNativeDialog
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], and if supported by the current [DisplayServer], OS native dialog will be used instead of custom one. [b]Note:[/b] On Android, it is only supported when using [constant ACCESS_FILESYSTEM]. For access mode [constant ACCESS_RESOURCES] and [constant ACCESS_USERDATA], the system will fall back to custom FileDialog. [b]Note:[/b] On Linux and macOS, sandboxed apps always use native dialogs to access the host file system. [b]Note:[/b] On macOS, sandboxed apps will save security-scoped bookmarks to retain access to the opened folders across multiple sessions. Use [method OS.get_granted_permissions] to get a list of saved bookmarks. [b]Note:[/b] Native dialogs are isolated from the base process, file dialog properties can't be modified once the dialog is shown. [b]Note:[/b] This property is ignored in [EditorFileDialog].

rawMembers.name: option_{index}/default
rawMembers.summary: The default value for the option at [code]index[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] [code]index[/code] is a value in the [code]0 .. option_count - 1[/code] range.

rawMembers.name: option_{index}/name
rawMembers.summary: The name of the option at [code]index[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] [code]index[/code] is a value in the [code]0 .. option_count - 1[/code] range.

rawMembers.name: option_{index}/values
rawMembers.summary: The list of values for the option at [code]index[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] [code]index[/code] is a value in the [code]0 .. option_count - 1[/code] range.

signals.name: DirSelected
signals.summary: Emitted when the user selects a directory.

signals.name: FileSelected
signals.summary: Emitted when the user selects a file by double-clicking it or pressing the [b]OK[/b] button.

signals.name: FilenameFilterChanged
signals.summary: Emitted when the filter for file names changes.

signals.name: FilesSelected
signals.summary: Emitted when the user selects multiple files.

values.name: ACCESS_FILESYSTEM
values.summary: The dialog allows accessing files on the whole file system.

values.name: ACCESS_RESOURCES
values.summary: The dialog only allows accessing files under the [Resource] path ([code]res://[/code]).

values.name: ACCESS_USERDATA
values.summary: The dialog only allows accessing files under user data path ([code]user://[/code]).

values.name: CUSTOMIZATION_CREATE_FOLDER
values.summary: If enabled, shows the button for creating new directories (when using [constant FILE_MODE_OPEN_DIR], [constant FILE_MODE_OPEN_ANY], or [constant FILE_MODE_SAVE_FILE]). Equivalent to [member folder_creation_enabled].

values.name: CUSTOMIZATION_DELETE
values.summary: If enabled, the context menu will show the "Delete" option, which allows moving files and folders to trash. Equivalent to [member deleting_enabled].

values.name: CUSTOMIZATION_FAVORITES
values.summary: If enabled, shows the toggle favorite button and favorite list on the left side of the dialog. Equivalent to [member favorites_enabled].

values.name: CUSTOMIZATION_FILE_FILTER
values.summary: If enabled, shows the toggle file filter button. Equivalent to [member file_filter_toggle_enabled].

values.name: CUSTOMIZATION_FILE_SORT
values.summary: If enabled, shows the file sorting options button. Equivalent to [member file_sort_options_enabled].

values.name: CUSTOMIZATION_HIDDEN_FILES
values.summary: Toggles visibility of the favorite button, and the favorite list on the left side of the dialog. Equivalent to [member hidden_files_toggle_enabled].

values.name: CUSTOMIZATION_LAYOUT
values.summary: If enabled, shows the layout switch buttons (list/thumbnails). Equivalent to [member layout_toggle_enabled].

values.name: CUSTOMIZATION_OVERWRITE_WARNING
values.summary: If enabled, the [FileDialog] will warn the user before overwriting files in save mode. Equivalent to [member overwrite_warning_enabled].

values.name: CUSTOMIZATION_RECENT
values.summary: If enabled, shows the recent directories list on the left side of the dialog. Equivalent to [member recent_list_enabled].

values.name: DISPLAY_LIST
values.summary: The dialog displays files as a list of filenames.

values.name: DISPLAY_THUMBNAILS
values.summary: The dialog displays files as a grid of thumbnails. Use [theme_item thumbnail_size] to adjust their size.

values.name: FILE_MODE_OPEN_ANY
values.summary: The dialog allows selecting one file or directory.

values.name: FILE_MODE_OPEN_DIR
values.summary: The dialog only allows selecting a directory, disallowing the selection of any file.

values.name: FILE_MODE_OPEN_FILE
values.summary: The dialog allows selecting one, and only one file.

values.name: FILE_MODE_OPEN_FILES
values.summary: The dialog allows selecting multiple files.

values.name: FILE_MODE_SAVE_FILE
values.summary: The dialog will warn when a file exists.

constants.name: FILE_MODE_OPEN_FILE
constants.summary: The dialog allows selecting one, and only one file.

constants.name: FILE_MODE_OPEN_FILES
constants.summary: The dialog allows selecting multiple files.

constants.name: FILE_MODE_OPEN_DIR
constants.summary: The dialog only allows selecting a directory, disallowing the selection of any file.

constants.name: FILE_MODE_OPEN_ANY
constants.summary: The dialog allows selecting one file or directory.

constants.name: FILE_MODE_SAVE_FILE
constants.summary: The dialog will warn when a file exists.

constants.name: ACCESS_RESOURCES
constants.summary: The dialog only allows accessing files under the [Resource] path ([code]res://[/code]).

constants.name: ACCESS_USERDATA
constants.summary: The dialog only allows accessing files under user data path ([code]user://[/code]).

constants.name: ACCESS_FILESYSTEM
constants.summary: The dialog allows accessing files on the whole file system.

constants.name: DISPLAY_THUMBNAILS
constants.summary: The dialog displays files as a grid of thumbnails. Use [theme_item thumbnail_size] to adjust their size.

constants.name: DISPLAY_LIST
constants.summary: The dialog displays files as a list of filenames.

constants.name: CUSTOMIZATION_HIDDEN_FILES
constants.summary: Toggles visibility of the favorite button, and the favorite list on the left side of the dialog. Equivalent to [member hidden_files_toggle_enabled].

constants.summary: If enabled, shows the button for creating new directories (when using [constant FILE_MODE_OPEN_DIR], [constant FILE_MODE_OPEN_ANY], or [constant FILE_MODE_SAVE_FILE]). Equivalent to [member folder_creation_enabled].

constants.name: CUSTOMIZATION_FILE_FILTER
constants.summary: If enabled, shows the toggle file filter button. Equivalent to [member file_filter_toggle_enabled].

constants.name: CUSTOMIZATION_FILE_SORT
constants.summary: If enabled, shows the file sorting options button. Equivalent to [member file_sort_options_enabled].

constants.name: CUSTOMIZATION_FAVORITES
constants.summary: If enabled, shows the toggle favorite button and favorite list on the left side of the dialog. Equivalent to [member favorites_enabled].

constants.name: CUSTOMIZATION_RECENT
constants.summary: If enabled, shows the recent directories list on the left side of the dialog. Equivalent to [member recent_list_enabled].

constants.name: CUSTOMIZATION_LAYOUT
constants.summary: If enabled, shows the layout switch buttons (list/thumbnails). Equivalent to [member layout_toggle_enabled].

constants.name: CUSTOMIZATION_OVERWRITE_WARNING
constants.summary: If enabled, the [FileDialog] will warn the user before overwriting files in save mode. Equivalent to [member overwrite_warning_enabled].

constants.name: CUSTOMIZATION_DELETE
